summary: Matched and typed a broad slice of menu-related code, especially `mn_2295` helpers, parts of `mnsoundtest`, and controller input plumbing in `gm_1A36`, while also adding several low-level math/library functions in `lb_00CE`. The PR renamed symbol `lb_8000CF74` to `powi`, added prototypes and struct definitions needed by menu code, refined audio/controller headers, and made small matching-oriented fixes in existing menu/stage code. Human discussion notes that the author was mainly interested in `powi` and `lb_8000D148`; the other math functions were included opportunistically, and `lb_8000D008` was suspected to look like `atan2` but was not fully analyzed.
what_changed: Renamed the symbol at `0x8000CF74` from `lb_8000CF74` to `powi` in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`.

Filled out `src/melee/lb/lb_00CE.c` with implementations for `expf`, `powf`, `powi`, `lb_8000D008`, and `lb_8000D148`; the author later clarified that not all of these were intentionally analyzed as final named math-library matches.
Added `lb_00CE.h` declarations for `expf`, `powf`, `powi`, `lb_8000D008`, and changed `lb_8000D148` to return `s32`.

Decompiled/typed multiple `gm_1A36` controller helper functions, including accessors for per-port `button`, `trigger`, and `unk` fields, event-bit propagation helpers, repeat/hold timer logic, and `gm_801A3A74` copying from `HSD_PadCopyStatus` into `gm_80479C30`.
Introduced `gm_controller_map` in `gm_1A36.static.h`, with `u64` fields for `button`, `trigger`, `repeat`, `release`, and `unk`, plus timer-like `s32 x28` and `x2C` fields.
Changed `gm_801A4B9C` from returning `s32` to returning `s8*`, with an explicit cast around `M2C_FIELD(gm_804D6720, s32*, 8)`.

Added `mn_2295.c` functions for detecting the first controller with the forward/menu-confirm bit, converting controller input masks into a `MenuEvent` bitfield, setting an `s8` value through `gm_801A4B9C`, and base-10 digit/string helper functions.

Expanded `MenuEvent` in `mn/inlines.h`, replacing `MenuEvent_Unk` with `MenuEvent_Forward`, keeping `MenuEvent_Back`, and adding several unknown event bits as `MenuEvent_unk` through `MenuEvent_unk6`.
Renamed menu sound inline helpers from `Menu_SFX_Back`, `Menu_SFX_Forward`, and `Menu_SFX_Move` to lower-camel-style `sfxBack`, `sfxForward`, and `sfxMove`.

Substantially expanded `mnsoundtest.c` with sound-test globals, Vec3 data, text/data lookup arrays, `soundtest_data`, and several display/update helper functions manipulating `HSD_Text`, `HSD_JObj`, and sound-test user data.
Moved `mnsoundtest` local externs/prototypes into headers where possible and refined `lbaudio_ax.h` prototypes for several audio functions used by menu/sound-test code.
Reworked `mndeflicker.c`, `mnhyaku.c`, and `mnsound.c` callsites to use the new menu event names and `sfxBack`/`sfxForward`/`sfxMove` helpers.
Made a small matching-oriented change in `grKongo_801D577C`, introducing a scoped `s16 val` temporary before decrementing `xCE`.
smart_moves: Defined a concrete `gm_controller_map` struct instead of continuing to pass around opaque controller state, which made the 64-bit menu input masks and repeat/trigger/release behavior much clearer.
Used `#pragma dont_inline on` around `gm_801A3A74`, indicating a matching tactic to preserve function boundaries while implementing controller-map aggregation logic.
Kept weakly understood menu event bits explicitly named as unknown variants (`MenuEvent_unk`, `MenuEvent_unk2`, etc.) rather than over-naming them without evidence.
Separated user-facing known menu event names (`Forward`, `Back`, `Up`, `Down`, `Left`, `Right`) from still-unknown bit meanings, improving future searchability without inventing semantics.
Added header prototypes and return types for audio and math helpers used by menu code, reducing reliance on `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` in downstream decompilation.
Preserved uncertainty in discussion: the author explicitly said `lb_8000D008` only looked like `atan2` and had not been fully analyzed.
decomp_lessons: Controller/menu input code in this area uses `u64` masks, including high bits such as `0x100000000`, so forcing these APIs into `u32` will hide important menu events.

When controller state is copied from `HSD_PadCopyStatus`, separate fields for `button`, `trigger`, `repeat`, and `release` are needed; menu helper functions then synthesize additional high-bit events from combinations of those fields.
For uncertain event semantics, prefer stable searchable placeholder names over speculative names; this PR used `MenuEvent_unk*` for unidentified bits while naming obvious `Forward` and `Back` events.

Math-like library functions can be landed with conservative names and comments/discussion documenting uncertainty. Here only `powi` was renamed in the symbol map, while `lb_8000D008` and `lb_8000D148` kept address-based names despite partial semantic clues.
Small type corrections, such as changing `gm_801A4B9C` to return `s8*`, can unlock cleaner menu helper implementations and remove misleading integer-pointer casts.
Data layout can be a matching obstacle in menu files; the `mnsoundtest` work used explicit Vec3 globals and lookup arrays rather than pretending all original data semantics were understood.
naming_conventions: Renamed a confirmed or strongly suspected library symbol in `symbols.txt`: `lb_8000CF74` became `powi`.
Kept uncertain library functions as address-based names, e.g. `lb_8000D008` and `lb_8000D148`, despite semantic hints.
Menu event enum names use `MenuEvent_` prefix with clear names for known directions/actions and `unk` suffixes for unknown bits.
Small inline menu SFX wrappers were renamed to concise lower-camel names: `sfxBack`, `sfxForward`, and `sfxMove`.
TU-private game-mode structs and globals were placed in `gm_1A36.static.h`, including `gm_controller_map`, `gm_80479C30`, and `controller_union`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, and `#pragma pop` around `gm_801A3A74` to avoid unwanted inlining during matching.
Used explicit `u64` shifts such as `((u64) 1 << (32 + n))` to construct high menu-event bits in `gm_801A3A74`.
Used an explicit temporary/scoped block in `grKongo_801D577C` (`s16 val = ...`) before decrementing a field, likely to match original load/compare ordering.
Kept an `M2C_FIELD` access in `gm_801A4B9C` but corrected the function return to `s8*`, balancing matching pragmatism with better typing.
The `powi` implementation uses an 8-multiply chunk loop followed by a remainder loop, preserving an unrolled structure likely relevant to matching.
The `mnsoundtest` data work used standalone Vec3 globals and arrays, plus a disabled `#if 0` aggregate Vec3 array, suggesting data-shape experimentation for matching.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Asked whether the added math-library functions were brand new math lib matches.
review_feedback.resolution_or_response: thefoxcam replied that they did not try to match all of them and were mainly interested in `powi` and `lb_8000D148`; `lb_8000D008` looked like `atan2` but was not analyzed long enough to confirm if it did anything else.

review_feedback.author: agent
review_feedback.feedback: No formal review comments were present in the supplied PR slice.
review_feedback.resolution_or_response: Review evidence is limited to two issue comments.

follow_up_queries: Investigate whether `lb_8000D008` should be named as an `atan2`-style helper or whether it has additional behavior.
Analyze and potentially name `lb_8000D148`; current evidence suggests a point/segment distance-threshold geometry test, but the PR did not rename it.
Resolve the semantics of `MenuEvent_unk` through `MenuEvent_unk6` once more menu callsites are matched.
Verify whether the `expf` and `powf` implementations in `lb_00CE.c` should be treated as final math-library matches or temporary decompilation output.
Continue typing `mnsoundtest` data structures and replace generic `unk` fields in `soundtest_user_data` when evidence becomes available.
Audit other `gm_1A36` controller APIs for `u64` mask correctness and high-bit event usage.
